instruction: In ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard, copy all files matching *failed.ipynb into ./fails while preserving directory hierarchy, but exclude any files under a tmp directory and any file whose basename starts with draft_. Then create ./fails/fails_manifest.txt containing sha256 and relative path (format: <sha256>  <relative_path_from_fails>) for copied .ipynb files only, sorted by relative path.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)
import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.write('cd ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard'); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.press('enter')

result.command: cd ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard; model_ok='false'; [ -f fails/projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb ] && model_ok='true'; eval_ok='false'; [ -f fails/projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb ] && eval_ok='true'; no_cache='false'; [ ! -f fails/projectA/tmp/cache_failed.ipynb ] && no_cache='true'; no_secret='false'; [ ! -f fails/projectB/tmp/deeper/secret_failed.ipynb ] && no_secret='true'; no_draft='false'; [ ! -f fails/projectB/deep/draft_experiment_failed.ipynb ] && no_draft='true'; manifest_ok='false'; [ -f fails/fails_manifest.txt ] && { expected=$(printf '%s\n' "$(sha256sum fails/projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb | awk '{print $1}')  projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb" "$(sha256sum fails/projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb | awk '{print $1}')  projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb" | sort); actual=$(cat fails/fails_manifest.txt 2>/dev/null); [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && manifest_ok='true'; }; echo "model_ok=$model_ok"; echo "eval_ok=$eval_ok"; echo "no_cache=$no_cache"; echo "no_secret=$no_secret"; echo "no_draft=$no_draft"; echo "manifest_ok=$manifest_ok"
